hero.heading: 🌳 About MyFamTreeCollab
hero.intro: Every family has a story. Sometimes it lives in memories, but often in boxes full of old documents.

origin.heading: 📦 How it started
origin.p1: This project started after I received a box of wedding, birth and death cards that my mother had kept for years. Throwing them away was not an option. But how do you preserve something like that in a way that truly has value?
origin.p2: What started in Excel quickly grew into a bigger insight: a family tree only becomes truly valuable when you can share it and build it together.

why.heading: 🤝 Why this website
why.p1: Many existing genealogy platforms are complex, closed or take control of your data. MyFamTreeCollab deliberately takes a different approach.
why.p2: You remain the owner of your data. You can save your family tree locally or in the cloud, and export it as a CSV, GEDCOM or JSON file. Everything you enter, including relationships, is fully contained in your file.

how.heading: 🧠 How it works
how.p1: You build your family tree using a simple structure centred around people and relationships. Parents, partners and children are displayed visually, giving you a quick overview of your family history.
how.p2: Unlike many other tools, your file is fully transparent and portable. No hidden structures, no lock-in.

local.heading: 🔄 Local or collaborative
local.p1: You can easily work locally and share your file with family. That is the simplest way to get started.
local.p2: Collaboration brings challenges such as version control and tracking changes. That is why a cloud solution is being developed to make collaboration easier and more manageable.

future.heading: 🚀 Future
future.p1: This project continues to evolve. The following additions are planned for the future:
future.p2: Linking photos to people, visualising family events, collaborating in a secure environment and communication between family members within the family tree.

why2.heading: ❤️ Why start
why2.p1: Because your family story is worth preserving. Because memories fade, but stories can live on.
why2.p2: A family tree is not built alone, but together.
